an5.114:1.1: At one time, the *Bhagavā* was dwelling among the Magadhans at Andhakavinda.
an5.114:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda approached the *Bhagavā*. Having approached, he paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side. Then the *Bhagavā* said this to Venerable Ānanda, who was sitting to one side:
an5.114:2.1: “Those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, who are new, recently gone forth, newly arrived in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] — those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, should be encouraged, settled, and established in five *dhammas*.
an5.114:2.3: ‘Come, friends, be virtuous, dwell restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, accomplished in conduct and proper resort, seeing danger in the slightest faults; undertake the training rules and train in them’—thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in restraint by the Pātimokkha.
an5.114:3.1: ‘Come, friends, dwell with the doors of the *indriyas* guarded, with protective *sati*, with prudent *sati*, with your mind well protected, endowed with a mind protected by *sati*’—thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in restraint of the *indriyas* [*indriyasaṁvare*].
an5.114:4.1: ‘Come, friends, speak little, keeping speech within limits’—thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in keeping speech within limits.
an5.114:5.1: ‘Come, friends, be forest-dwellers, resort to remote lodgings in forests and wild woodland places’—thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in bodily seclusion.
an5.114:6.1: ‘Come, friends, have right view, endowed with right seeing’—thus they should be encouraged, settled, and established in right seeing.
an5.114:6.2: Those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, who are new, recently gone forth, newly arrived in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinayaṁ*] — those *bhikkhus*, Ānanda, should be encouraged, settled, and established in these five *dhammas*.”
